fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 69 uses strict greater-than (`now > adjusted_expiry`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= adjusted_expiry`), so a token whose current time exactly equals its adjusted expiry is incorrectly treated as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: When now equals adjusted_expiry, the branch should treat the token as expired (return None), so the condition must use >= not >.
After computing adjusted_expiry, verify that when now >= adjusted_expiry the function will correctly return None (expiry boundary inclusive check).
If execution falls through the expiry check (token returned as valid), now must be strictly less than adjusted_expiry; equality should have triggered expiry.
Confirm adjusted_expiry is derived from expires_at so the boundary comparison at line 69 applies to the correct value.
